models.name: llama3.2:3b
models.systemPrompt: You are a careful clinical decision-support assistant. Reply with the single most likely diagnosis in a few words. If the information given is not sufficient to determine one diagnosis, reply exactly: INSUFFICIENT INFORMATION.

transcripts.prompt: A 19-year-old has 2 days of intense thirst, frequent urination, and vomiting. Blood glucose is 512 mg/dL, arterial pH 7.18, and serum ketones are positive. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 19-year-old has 2 days of intense thirst, frequent urination, and vomiting. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 20-year-old has fever, severe headache, and neck stiffness. Lumbar puncture shows CSF with 4,000 neutrophils/uL, glucose 20 mg/dL, and protein 250 mg/dL. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 20-year-old has fever, severe headache, and neck stiffness. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 58-year-old has 40 minutes of crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm. The ECG shows 3 mm ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 58-year-old has 40 minutes of crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 42-year-old has fatigue and pallor. Hemoglobin is 9 g/dL, MCV 68 fL (microcytic), and serum ferritin 6 ng/mL. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 42-year-old has fatigue and pallor. Hemoglobin is 9 g/dL. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 50-year-old has fatigue, cold intolerance, and weight gain. TSH is 14 mIU/L and free T4 is low. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 50-year-old has fatigue, cold intolerance, and weight gain. What is the single most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old has an acutely painful, red, swollen first metatarsophalangeal (big-toe) joint. Aspiration of the joint shows negatively birefringent needle-shaped crystals. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 55-year-old has an acutely painful, red, swollen first metatarsophalangeal (big-toe) joint. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A dialysis patient has weakness and palpitations. Serum potassium is 7.2 mmol/L and the ECG shows tall peaked T waves. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A dialysis patient has weakness and palpitations. What is the single most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old woman has 2 days of dysuria and urinary frequency. Urinalysis is positive for nitrites and leukocyte esterase, and culture grows greater than 10^5 CFU/mL of E. coli. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 28-year-old woman has 2 days of dysuria and urinary frequency. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 65-year-old has fever and a productive cough. Chest X-ray shows a right lower lobe consolidation. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 65-year-old has fever and a productive cough. What is the single most likely diagnosis?

A 22-year-old has one day of abdominal pain that started periumbilically and migrated to the right lower quadrant, with tenderness at McBurney's point. CT shows a dilated appendix with wall thickening and fat stranding. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 22-year-old has one day of abdominal pain and nausea. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 47-year-old has a sudden 'worst headache of my life' that peaked in seconds. Non-contrast head CT shows blood in the basal cisterns. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 47-year-old has a sudden severe headache. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A diabetic on insulin becomes confused and diaphoretic. Fingerstick glucose is 38 mg/dL, and symptoms resolve promptly after intravenous dextrose. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A diabetic on insulin becomes confused and diaphoretic. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 19-year-old has 2 days of intense thirst, frequent urination, and vomiting. Blood glucose is 512 mg/dL, arterial pH 7.18, and serum ketones are positive. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
A 19-year-old has 2 days of intense thirst, frequent urination, and vomiting. What is the single most likely diagnosis?
transcripts.response: INSUFFICIENT - the decisive lab values are not given.
